name: TxModel/RxModel use Transmitter/Receiver wrappers
description: Verifies SerdesSystem is constructed with Transmitter/Receiver objects, not cell arrays
prompt: Create a SerdesSystem with a 3-tap FFE transmitter and a CTLE + DFECDR receiver for a 28 Gbps NRZ link.

assertions.description: Generated SerdesSystem code runs without error in MATLAB
TxModel constructed with Transmitter wrapper
RxModel constructed with Receiver wrapper
Does not assign cell array directly to TxModel or RxModel

name: ChannelData uses correct property names
description: Verifies ChannelData configured with ChannelLossdB, ChannelLossFreq, ChannelDifferentialImpedance
prompt: Set up a SerdesSystem with a channel that has 12 dB loss at 14 GHz Nyquist frequency and 100 ohm differential impedance.

assertions.description: Generated code with ChannelData configuration runs in MATLAB
Uses ChannelLossdB property
Uses ChannelLossFreq property
Does not use wrong property names like .Loss or .TargetFrequency

name: CTLE Specification set before ACGain
description: Verifies CTLE Specification is set to 'DC Gain and AC Gain' before or with ACGain
prompt: Create a CTLE equalizer with 0 dB DC gain and 12 dB AC gain for a PAM4 receiver.

assertions.description: Generated CTLE construction code runs without error
Specification set to 'DC Gain and AC Gain' before or simultaneously with ACGain
Uses the DC Gain and AC Gain specification
Does not set ACGain without correct Specification

name: Mode=2 only on CTLE/DFECDR/DFE
description: Verifies agent refuses Mode=2 on FFE and AGC, recommends Mode=1 instead
prompt: I want to export my FFE and AGC blocks as adaptive AMI parameters. Set Mode=2 on both so they can converge at runtime.

assertions.description: Does not set Mode=2 on serdes.FFE
Does not set Mode=2 on serdes.AGC
States only CTLE, DFECDR, and DFE support Mode=2
Suggests Mode=1 as alternative for FFE and AGC

name: Adaptive blocks require Dual model
description: Verifies agent identifies conflict between Init-Only model and Mode=2 adaptive blocks
prompt: Create an Init-Only IBIS-AMI Rx model that uses DFECDR with Mode=2 for adaptive equalization.

assertions.description: Identifies that Mode=2 requires a Dual model
Does not create an Init-Only model with Mode=2 blocks without flagging the conflict
Explains Init-Only models cannot run GetWave so DFE taps never converge
Recommends Init_Returns_Impulse=true AND GetWave_Exists=true

name: Column vectors for signal conversion functions
description: Verifies impulse2pulse and pulse2stateye receive column vectors, not row vectors
prompt: Convert an impulse response to a pulse response using impulse2pulse, then compute the statistical eye with pulse2stateye. Use a 128-sample impulse at 16 samples per symbol.

assertions.description: Generated signal conversion code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates impulse as column vector: zeros(128, 1) or equivalent
Does not create row vector impulse
Uses column-oriented indexing throughout (N-by-1)

name: JitterAndNoise Rj is standard deviation
description: Verifies Tx_Rj set as RMS standard deviation and Tx_Dj as peak-to-peak per IBIS 7.0
prompt: Add jitter and noise to my SerdesSystem with 2 ps RMS random jitter and 8 ps peak-to-peak deterministic jitter on the transmitter.

assertions.description: Generated JitterAndNoise code runs without error
Sets Tx_Rj = 2e-12 as standard deviation
Sets Tx_Dj = 8e-12 as peak-to-peak
Mentions Rj is standard deviation per IBIS 7.0

name: No fabricated IBIS read/write functions
description: Verifies agent does not call readIBIS, exportAMI, or generateAMI; explains Simulink model is required
prompt: Read an existing .ibs file into MATLAB and export my SerdesSystem directly to an .ami file.

assertions.description: Does not call readIBIS (does not exist)
Does not call exportAMI (does not exist)
Explains IBIS-AMI generation requires a Simulink model
Mentions exportToSimulink as the first step

name: WaveType set explicitly in MATLAB
description: Verifies WaveType is set to 'Impulse' when processing impulse data in standalone MATLAB
prompt: Apply a 3-tap FFE to an impulse response in MATLAB standalone (not in Simulink). The impulse is 200 samples long.

assertions.description: Sets WaveType to 'Impulse' before processing
Does not rely on default WaveType ('Sample') for impulse data
Sets SymbolTime and SampleInterval on the block
Explains Simulink sets WaveType automatically but MATLAB requires explicit setting

name: Char vectors for Transmitter constructor
description: Verifies Transmitter uses single-quoted 'Blocks' (char vector), not double-quoted string
prompt: Build a SerdesSystem with a Transmitter containing an FFE and a Receiver containing a CTLE for a 56 Gbps PAM4 link.

assertions.description: Generated PAM4 SerdesSystem code runs without error
Uses Transmitter('Blocks', ...) with single-quoted char vector
Uses Receiver('Blocks', ...) with single-quoted char vector
Does not use double-quoted string for Transmitter property name

name: analysis() returns Nx2 matrices
description: Verifies equalized pulse extracted from column 2 of results.pulse, not treated as vector
prompt: Run analysis on my SerdesSystem, extract the equalized pulse response, and compute eye metrics with optPulseMetric.

assertions.description: Generated analysis and metric extraction code runs without error
Extracts equalized pulse from column 2
Does not pass results.pulse directly to optPulseMetric
Mentions column 1 is unequalized and column 2 is equalized

name: optPulseMetric is LTI-only
description: Verifies agent recommends sys.Metrics.summary for jitter-aware metrics instead of optPulseMetric
prompt: I set JitterAndNoise on my system. Now I want the jitter-aware eye height. Should I use optPulseMetric?

assertions.description: States optPulseMetric is LTI-only
Recommends sys.Metrics.summary.EH for jitter-aware eye height
Does not recommend optPulseMetric for jitter-aware metrics
Mentions analysis(sys) must be called first to populate Metrics

name: plotStatEye renders into current axes
description: Verifies plotStatEye is used correctly with title() and optional multi-panel layouts
prompt: Plot the statistical eye diagram for my SerdesSystem and add a custom title 'PCIe Gen5 Rx Eye'.

assertions.description: Uses title() after plotStatEye to label the axes
Calls plotStatEye(sys) before title
Creates a figure before plotting

name: serdes.AMI called with 3 arguments
description: Verifies serdes.AMI is called with (waveIn, impulseIn, clockIn), not just impulseIn
prompt: Validate a compiled Rx IBIS-AMI DLL by running its AMI_Init function with a channel impulse response.

assertions.description: Calls ami(waveIn, impulseIn, clockIn) with exactly 3 inputs
Does not call ami with only 1 argument
Uses zeros(N,1) or scalar 0 as dummy waveIn for Init-only
Captures both outputs: [waveOut, impulseOut] or [~, impulseOut]

name: clockIn = -1 when not clock-forwarding
description: Verifies clockIn set to -1 for non-clock-forwarding AMI validation
prompt: Run the Init function of my compiled AMI model to check its impulse response. I am not using clock forwarding.

assertions.description: Uses clockIn = -1 when not clock-forwarding
Does not omit clockIn argument
Does not use clockIn = 0 or clockIn = []
Sets InitOnly = true for Init-only validation

name: serdes.AMIExport for R2026a+
description: Verifies serdes.AMIExport used for programmatic AMI generation on R2026a+
prompt: I am on R2026a. How do I programmatically generate AMI files and DLLs from my Simulink SerDes model without using the GUI?

assertions.description: Mentions serdes.AMIExport as the recommended approach
Shows creating AMIExport object from Simulink model name
Sets DLLFiles = true to generate compiled DLLs
Calls export(AMIExport) to trigger generation

name: slbuild for R2025b and earlier
description: Verifies slbuild workflow used for AMI generation on R2025b and earlier
prompt: I am on R2025b. How do I programmatically build AMI DLLs from my Simulink SerDes model?

assertions.description: Uses slbuild with model/Tx and model/Rx subsystem paths
Mentions exportToSimulink as prerequisite
Notes Simulink Coder and Embedded Coder are required
Does not recommend serdes.AMIExport (R2026a+ only)

name: Custom blocks inherit SerdesAbstractSystemObject
description: Verifies custom datapath blocks inherit from serdes.SerdesAbstractSystemObject, not matlab.System
prompt: Create a custom datapath block for my SerDes system that implements a non-linear saturation model.

assertions.description: Inherits from serdes.SerdesAbstractSystemObject
Does not inherit from matlab.System directly
Declares IsLinear and IsTimeInvariant properties
Implements stepImpl method

name: Custom block dispatches on WaveType
description: Verifies custom block stepImpl dispatches on WaveType for Impulse vs Sample processing
prompt: Write a custom SerDes datapath block that handles both impulse-domain and sample-by-sample processing correctly.

assertions.description: stepImpl checks WaveType to branch between Impulse and Sample
Handles WaveType = 'Impulse' for Init path
Handles WaveType = 'Sample' for GetWave path
Inherits from serdes.SerdesAbstractSystemObject

name: Lists .so alongside .dll deliverables
description: Verifies agent mentions Linux .so alongside Windows .dll as IBIS-AMI deliverables
prompt: What files does the IBIS-AMI generation process produce? I need to deliver models to our EDA team.

assertions.description: Mentions .so (Linux shared object)
Mentions .dll (Windows DLL)
Mentions .ami and .ibs files
Mentions cross-compilation for Linux

name: SParameterChannel with FileName name-value
description: Verifies SParameterChannel uses FileName NV pair and feeds ImpulseResponse into ChannelData constructor
prompt: I have a measured channel in a .s4p Touchstone file. Load it into my SerdesSystem for analysis.

assertions.description: Generated S-parameter loading code runs without error
Uses SParameterChannel with FileName name-value pair, not positional
Feeds ImpulseResponse into ChannelData constructor, not post-hoc assignment
Sets SampleInterval and StopTime on SParameterChannel object

name: Crosstalk ICN configuration
description: Verifies crosstalk configured with EnableCrosstalk, Custom spec, and FEXTICN/NEXTICN in volts
prompt: Add FEXT and NEXT crosstalk to my loss-model channel. I want 15 mV FEXT and 10 mV NEXT.

assertions.description: Sets EnableCrosstalk = true on ChannelData
Sets CrosstalkSpecification to Custom
Sets FEXTICN = 0.015 and NEXTICN = 0.010 in volts RMS
Does not fabricate a Crosstalk object or addAggressor method

name: AMI InputString uses default root name
description: Verifies InputString uses serdes_rx default root name, not Simulink model name
prompt: I compiled my SerDes model to AMI DLLs. Now I want to validate the Rx DLL with serdes.AMI. How do I set up the InputString?

assertions.description: Uses InputString = '(serdes_rx)' with correct default root name
Mentions generateDefaultInputString for third-party .ami files
Sets InitOnly and clockIn = -1 for Init-only validation
Does not fabricate InputString in XML, JSON, or struct format

name: Simulink parsim sweep workflow
description: Verifies parsim sweep uses SimulationInput, string parameter values, and save_system
prompt: I exported my SerdesSystem to Simulink. Now I want to sweep CTLE AC gain from 0 to 20 dB using parsim. Show me the workflow.

assertions.description: Creates Simulink.SimulationInput array with setBlockParameter
Passes parameter values as strings via num2str or equivalent
Calls save_system before parsim
Does not fabricate sweep functions like simSweep or parameterSweep

name: End-to-end IBIS-AMI generation workflow
description: Verifies the complete path from SerdesSystem design through DLL generation and validation
prompt: Walk me through the complete process of creating an IBIS-AMI Tx/Rx model pair from scratch. I have a 56 Gbps PAM4 link with a 3-tap FFE transmitter and CTLE + adaptive DFE receiver. I need to produce .ami, .ibs, and .dll files and then validate the compiled models match the behavioral simulation.

assertions.description: Step 1: Creates SerdesSystem with Transmitter/Receiver wrappers and correct PAM4 settings
Step 2: Calls exportToSimulink(sys) to create Simulink model
Step 3: Configures and generates via IbisAmiManager or serdes.AMIExport or slbuild
Step 4: Validates compiled DLLs using serdes.AMI with correlation check against behavioral reference
Sets ModelTypeRx to Dual model because DFECDR uses Mode=2 adaptive
Mentions .ami, .ibs, and .dll/.so as output deliverables

name: AMI parameter export flags use *AMI suffix
description: Verifies get_param/set_param for AMI parameters use the *AMI suffix convention
prompt: I want to check which block parameters will be exported to the AMI file, and disable the FFE Mode parameter from being exported. How do I do this programmatically?

assertions.description: Uses get_param with *AMI suffix to query export status
Uses set_param with ModeAMI to disable FFE Mode export
Does not fabricate non-existent set_param parameter names like AMIVersion or ExportAMI
Shows the block path format: model/Tx/FFE or model/Rx/CTLE

name: Behavioral vs compiled AMI correlation validation
description: Verifies the pattern for comparing behavioral analysis results against compiled DLL output
prompt: I built my AMI DLLs. How do I verify the compiled Tx and Rx models produce the same impulse response as my behavioral SerdesSystem simulation?

assertions.description: Gets behavioral reference from analysis(sys) — results.impulse(:,2) for equalized
Chains Tx AMI Init output into Rx AMI Init input
Uses corrcoef or similar to compare behavioral vs compiled output
Sets RowSize = N to match impulse response length

name: Redriver IBIS-AMI model configuration
description: Verifies redriver (linear repeater without CDR) is configured correctly with AMIExport
prompt: I need to build an IBIS-AMI model for a redriver — a linear repeater with CTLE and VGA but no CDR. How do I configure this?

assertions.description: Uses serdes.VGA for variable gain amplification
Does not include DFECDR or CDR in Rx blocks (no clock recovery in redriver)
Sets ModelConfiguration to Redriver on AMIExport
Does not include DFECDR in receiver for a redriver

name: CTLE PeakingFrequency set to Nyquist
description: Verifies PeakingFrequency is set to Nyquist = 1/(2*SymbolTime), not left at default 5 GHz
prompt: Configure a CTLE for a 56 Gbps PAM4 link with 0 dB DC gain and 10 dB AC gain. The symbol time is 35.71 ps.

assertions.description: Generated CTLE code runs without error
Sets PeakingFrequency to ~14 GHz (Nyquist for 56 Gbps PAM4)
Does not leave PeakingFrequency at default 5 GHz
Sets Specification to 'DC Gain and AC Gain' to enable ACGain

name: ChannelData Impulse via constructor not post-hoc
description: Verifies impulse response passed via ChannelData constructor, not assigned after construction
prompt: I have an impulse response vector from my own channel simulation. How do I feed it into SerdesSystem instead of using the built-in loss model?

assertions.description: Creates ChannelData with Impulse and dt as constructor name-value pairs
Does not assign impulse post-hoc via sys.ChannelData.Impulse = ...
Passes dt matching the system's SampleInterval
Passes ChannelData object into SerdesSystem constructor

name: eyeDiagramSI called without output capture
description: Verifies eyeDiagramSI step called without capturing output, metrics read via eyeHeight()
prompt: Create an eye diagram from a waveform using eyeDiagramSI and measure the eye height.

assertions.description: Calls eye(waveform) without capturing output — no 'eye = eye(waveform)'
Does not assign output: eye = eye(waveform) would error
Uses eyeHeight(eye) to read the eye height metric after stepping
Sets SampleInterval, SymbolTime, and Modulation before stepping

name: DFECDR requires sample-by-sample loop in Sample mode
description: Verifies DFECDR in Sample mode is called one sample at a time in a loop, not with full vector
prompt: Process a waveform through a DFECDR equalizer in MATLAB standalone using Sample mode with 5 adaptive DFE taps.

assertions.description: Processes samples one at a time in a for loop
Does not pass full waveform vector to DFECDR in Sample mode
Sets WaveType to 'Sample' and Mode to 2 for adaptive
Sets SymbolTime and SampleInterval on the DFECDR object

name: ctlefit to GPZ Matrix CTLE workflow
description: Verifies ctlefit used to extract GPZ matrix from transfer function data for CTLE configuration
prompt: I have CTLE transfer function measurements in a CSV file. How do I fit poles and zeros and use them to configure a serdes.CTLE block?

assertions.description: Uses ctlefit.readcsv to import transfer function data
Creates ctlefit object with f, H, and SampleInterval
Extracts GPZ matrix from fitted object
Creates CTLE with Specification='GPZ Matrix' and GPZ from ctlefit

name: set_param values must be strings
description: Verifies set_param block parameters passed as strings, not numeric values
prompt: I want to programmatically change the CTLE AC gain to 12 dB and FFE tap weights to [-0.1 0.8 -0.1] in my Simulink SerDes model.

assertions.description: Uses num2str for scalar parameters like ACGain
Uses mat2str for array parameters like TapWeights
Does not pass numeric values directly to set_param
Uses correct block path format: model/Rx/CTLE, model/Tx/FFE

name: Design space sweep with analysis() and COMestimate
description: Verifies agent creates a parameter sweep loop using analysis() and extracts COM from sys.Metrics.summary.COMestimate
prompt: Sweep CTLE AC gain from 0 to 14 dB in steps of 2 dB for a 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem with 15 dB channel loss. Find the optimal AC gain that maximizes COM.

assertions.description: Creates SerdesSystem inside or before the sweep loop with correct PAM4 settings
Calls analysis(sys) for each sweep iteration
Reads COM from sys.Metrics.summary.COMestimate, not optPulseMetric
Sets PeakingFrequency to ~14 GHz (Nyquist for PAM4 at 28 GBaud)
Does not fabricate a sweep function like serdesSweep or analysisSweep

name: Jitter decomposition with jitter() function
description: Verifies agent uses jitter() with correct field names (TJrms, DDJpkpk) and Plot=false
prompt: I have an equalized waveform from my SerDes system. Decompose its jitter into random, deterministic, data-dependent, and ISI components. I need the results in a script, not interactively.

assertions.description: Uses jitter() function with SampleInterval and SymbolTime name-value pairs
Sets Plot=false to suppress automatic histogram figures
Accesses fields with rms/pkpk suffixes (TJrms, TJpkpk, RJrms, DDJpkpk, ISIpkpk)
Does not use plain field names like J.TJ or J.RJ (these do not exist)
Generates a sufficiently long waveform using pulse2wave with prbs for ISI decomposition

name: Full waveform processing chain: equalize, analyze, jitter
description: Verifies agent builds the complete waveform processing workflow: FFE → CTLE → DFECDR loop → eyeDiagramSI → jitter
prompt: I have a captured waveform from a 10 Gbps NRZ link. Stream it through FFE, CTLE, and adaptive DFECDR equalization in MATLAB, then build an eye diagram and decompose jitter on the equalized result.

assertions.description: Sets WaveType to 'Sample' on all blocks and sets SymbolTime/SampleInterval
Processes DFECDR sample-by-sample in a for loop, not with full vector
Creates eyeDiagramSI and calls step without output capture, then reads eyeHeight
Calls jitter() on equalized waveform with Plot=false
Does not pass full waveform vector to DFECDR in Sample mode

name: Read adapted DFE taps from results.outparams
description: Verifies adapted DFE taps are read from results.outparams, not from the block object which retains initial values
prompt: I built a SerdesSystem with a 10-tap adaptive DFECDR (Mode=2) and ran analysis(). How do I read the adapted DFE tap weights that the solver computed?

assertions.description: Reads adapted taps from results.outparams, not sys.RxModel.Blocks{k}.TapWeights
Does not read taps from the block object (which still holds initial values)
Accesses the DFECDR struct field within outparams cell array
Extracts TapWeights from the DFECDR outparams struct

name: Correct Metrics.summary field names and units
description: Verifies agent uses correct field names (EH, EW, COMestimate) with correct units, not fabricated names like maxEyeHeight
prompt: After running analysis() on my 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem, I want to extract eye height, eye width, and COM from the statistical analysis results. Print them in mV and ps.

assertions.description: Uses sys.Metrics.summary.EH for eye height
Uses sys.Metrics.summary.EW for eye width
Multiplies EH by 1e3 for mV (EH is in volts)
Does NOT multiply EW by 1e12 (EW is already in picoseconds)
Does not use fabricated field names like maxEyeHeight or maxEyeWidth

name: Full system construction with AnalogModel and JitterAndNoise
description: Verifies SerdesSystem includes AnalogModel on Tx/Rx and JitterAndNoise for realistic results
prompt: Create a complete, production-quality 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem with FFE, CTLE, and adaptive DFE. Use a 15 dB loss channel. I need realistic COM numbers.

assertions.description: Includes AnalogModel on Transmitter with R and C parameters
Includes AnalogModel on Receiver with R and C parameters
Includes JitterAndNoise object in SerdesSystem construction
Sets Signaling to Differential
Generated SerdesSystem code runs without error in MATLAB

name: DFE sweep reads adapted taps from outparams
description: Verifies a loss sweep that reports DFE taps reads them from outparams, not block objects
prompt: Sweep channel loss from 10 to 30 dB in 5 dB steps for a PAM4 system with 10-tap adaptive DFE. Report COM and the adapted DFE tap weights at each loss level.

assertions.description: Creates a sweep loop with SerdesSystem and analysis() at each step
Reads adapted DFE taps from results.outparams, not block object
Searches outparams cell array for the DFECDR struct entry
Reports COM from sys.Metrics.summary.COMestimate

name: Side-by-side eye comparison uses tiledlayout or subplot
description: Verifies plotStatEye used in a multi-panel layout with title() for labeling
prompt: Create a script that compares two SerdesSystem configurations side by side: one with 10 dB channel loss and one with 20 dB. Show the statistical eye for each and print COM and eye height. Use a 56 Gbps PAM4 link with FFE, CTLE, and adaptive DFE.

assertions.description: Uses tiledlayout/nexttile or subplot for multi-panel layout
Uses title() to label each plotStatEye panel
Reads metrics from sys.Metrics.summary with correct field names (EH, EW, COMestimate)
Includes AnalogModel and JitterAndNoise in system construction

name: Comparison script writes .m file with correct patterns
description: Verifies a comparison/sweep script uses correct patterns: tiledlayout (not subplot), outparams for DFE, EH in volts, EW in ps
prompt: Write a MATLAB script that sweeps channel loss from 10 to 25 dB in 5 dB steps for a 56 Gbps PAM4 system with FFE + CTLE + 10-tap adaptive DFE. For each loss level, plot the statistical eye, report COM, eye height in mV, eye width in ps, and the adapted DFE tap weights.

assertions.description: Writes code to a .m file, not inline evaluation
Reads adapted DFE taps from results.outparams, not block object
Converts EH to mV by multiplying by 1e3 (EH is in volts)
Uses EW directly without scaling (already in picoseconds)
Uses tiledlayout/nexttile or subplot for multi-panel eye layout
Includes AnalogModel on Tx and Rx, JitterAndNoise, and Signaling='Differential'

name: GPZ ConfigSelect requires Mode=1 to switch rows
description: Verifies that when using GPZ Matrix specification with ConfigSelect, Mode is set to 1 (not left at default 0)
prompt: I have a GPZ matrix with 6 rows from ctlefit. I want to sweep ConfigSelect from 0 to 5 to compare all 6 CTLE configurations in my SerdesSystem. Show me how to set up the CTLE block.

assertions.description: Sets Specification to 'GPZ Matrix'
Sets Mode=1 to enable ConfigSelect row switching
Uses ConfigSelect (0-indexed) to select GPZ rows
Does not leave Mode at default 0 (which ignores ConfigSelect)

name: Rx clock recovery jitter requires RxClockMode=clocked
description: Verifies that when configuring Rx clock recovery jitter, RxClockMode is set to clocked (not ideal)
prompt: I want to model CDR jitter on my 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem. Add 0.5 ps RMS random jitter and 2 ps peak-to-peak deterministic jitter on the Rx clock recovery. Show me how to configure JitterAndNoise.

assertions.description: Sets RxClockMode to 'clocked' (not 'ideal')
Sets Rx_Clock_Recovery_Rj to 0.5e-12 (seconds, not UI)
Sets Rx_Clock_Recovery_Dj to 2e-12 (seconds, not UI)
Does not use Type='UI' (silently ignored by analysis)

name: Jitter from UI spec converted correctly
description: Verifies jitter from a UI spec is configured correctly — either via manual conversion (multiply by symbolTime) or SimpleJitter with Type='UI' and Include=true
prompt: A spec says my Tx has 0.05 UI of random jitter and 0.1 UI of deterministic jitter. Configure JitterAndNoise for a 56 Gbps PAM4 system (28 GBaud).

assertions.description: References symbolTime for UI-to-seconds conversion or uses SimpleJitter Type='UI'
Sets Tx_Rj and Tx_Dj jitter properties
If using SimpleJitter, sets Include=true (defaults to false otherwise jitter is ignored)

name: Multi-port S-parameter files include aggressors automatically
description: Verifies multi-port files (.s8p/.s12p/.s16p) are loaded with SParameterChannel and full ImpulseResponse matrix passed to ChannelData
prompt: Load a 12-port S-parameter file (channel_20dB_2agg.s12p) with 2 aggressors into a 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem and run analysis including the crosstalk from the aggressors.

assertions.description: Uses SParameterChannel with FileName name-value pair
Passes full ImpulseResponse matrix (not just column 1) to ChannelData constructor
Uses ChannelData constructor with Impulse and dt name-value pairs
Does not try to set EnableCrosstalk for S-parameter aggressors (auto-included)

name: Custom crosstalk uses correct ICN configuration
description: Verifies Custom crosstalk sets EnableCrosstalk, CrosstalkSpecification, FEXTICN, NEXTICN, and fb correctly
prompt: Add crosstalk to a 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem with 15 dB loss. Use custom ICN values: FEXT = 0.02 V RMS and NEXT = 0.005 V RMS.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets EnableCrosstalk to true
Sets CrosstalkSpecification to Custom
Sets FEXTICN value
Sets NEXTICN value

name: AnalogModel includes realistic parasitic capacitance
description: Verifies Tx and Rx AnalogModel are configured with R and C values for realistic termination modeling
prompt: Build a 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem with realistic analog models. The Tx has 50 ohm termination with 100 fF parasitic capacitance. The Rx has 50 ohm termination with 200 fF parasitic capacitance. Include appropriate rise time.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Tx AnalogModel constructed with R and C parameters
Rx AnalogModel constructed with R and C parameters
RiseTime set on Transmitter (approximately 0.3-0.4 × SymbolTime)

name: Transmitter constructor uses single-quoted property names
description: Verifies Transmitter is constructed with char vector (single quote) property names, not string (double quote) names which cause an ismember error
prompt: Create a 56 Gbps PAM4 SerdesSystem with a 3-tap FFE transmitter with AnalogModel and a CTLE + DFECDR receiver. Use 15 dB channel loss.

assertions.description: Generated SerdesSystem code runs without error in MATLAB
Transmitter uses single-quoted property names (char vectors)
Does not use double-quoted string for Transmitter property names

name: GA-based parameter optimization uses correct formulation
description: Verifies agent sets up Genetic Algorithm optimization for SerDes parameters with proper constraints, quantization, and fitness function
prompt: Optimize 3-tap FFE weights and CTLE AC gain jointly for a 56 Gbps PAM4 system at 20 dB channel loss. Use a genetic algorithm. The FFE taps should be quantized to 0.02 resolution. Show me the optimal settings and COM.

assertions.description: Uses ga() from Global Optimization Toolbox
Defines bounds for all optimization variables
assertions.expected: lb and ub or lower bound and upper bound

assertions.description: Fitness function uses analysis() with Metrics.summary for COM or EH
Transmitter uses single-quoted property names in fitness function
Optimizes multiple parameters jointly (not just FFE alone)
assertions.expected: ACGain or acGain as optimization variable

name: Explains EH vs COM tradeoff for optimization fitness metric
description: Verifies agent understands and explains the difference between optimizing for eye height vs COM
prompt: I want to optimize my SerDes equalization settings for a 56 Gbps PAM4 link. Should I use COM or eye height as the optimization metric? What's the difference?

assertions.description: Explains COM is compliance-oriented (signal-to-noise ratio)
Explains eye height measures voltage margin
Notes that EH and COM can produce different optimal parameters
assertions.expected: diverge or different optimal or different results

assertions.description: For PAM4 EH, uses min() across eyes (worst-eye metric)
assertions.expected: min(EH) or worst eye or minimum eye

name: Multi-panel layout with SerdesSystem plot methods
description: Verifies agent uses plotImpulse/plotPulse/plotAlignedPulse/plotWavePattern with tiledlayout or subplot
prompt: Plot the impulse response and pulse response side by side for my SerdesSystem, so I can compare the unequalized and equalized shapes in one figure.

assertions.description: Uses plotImpulse(sys) for the impulse response panel
Uses plotPulse(sys) for the pulse response panel
Uses tiledlayout/nexttile or subplot for multi-panel layout
Uses title() to label each panel

name: Stimulus block jitter units default to UI
description: Verifies agent sets jitter unit to Seconds when configuring Simulink Stimulus block with absolute jitter values via set_param
prompt: Write a MATLAB script that configures the Simulink Stimulus block to use Serial PRBS with 1 ps RMS random jitter and 3 ps deterministic jitter via set_param.

assertions.description: Sets soRjUnit to Seconds before or alongside setting soRj
Sets soDjUnit to Seconds before or alongside setting soDj
Uses so prefix for Stimulus mask parameters
Does not set jitter values without overriding units to Seconds

name: VEC units differ between Metrics.summary and eyeDiagramSI
description: Verifies agent correctly reports VEC units when using both statistical and waveform analysis APIs
prompt: Analyze a 56 Gbps PAM4 SerDes system using both statistical analysis (analysis/Metrics.summary) and time-domain eye diagram (eyeDiagramSI). Report COM, EH, EW, and VEC from both paths with correct units.

assertions.description: Reports Metrics.summary.VEC in percent (not dB)
Reports eyeDiagramSI vec() in dB (not percent)
Reports EW from Metrics.summary without multiplying by 1e12 (already in ps)
Reports eyeWidth from eyeDiagramSI in seconds, converting to ps with 1e12

name: Load captured waveform and equalize with CTLE+DFE
description: Verifies agent can load a .mat waveform file, set up system objects EQ chain, and measure eye metrics
prompt: I have a captured NRZ waveform in data/waveforms/nrz_28GBaud_20dB.mat. The file contains rxWaveform (the signal), plus metadata with baudRate, dt, samplesPerSymbol, and adaptedDfeTaps. Load it and equalize with CTLE (8 dB AC gain) + DFE using the pre-loaded taps from metadata. Measure the eye height and COM with eyeDiagramSI.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error
Sets CTLE Specification to 'DC Gain and AC Gain' before ACGain
Sets WaveType='Sample' on EQ blocks for waveform processing
Uses Mode=1 (fixed taps) when loading pre-adapted taps
DFE applied sample-by-sample in a loop
Uses eyeDiagramSI for waveform eye measurement

name: PAM4 waveform with adaptive DFE requires Modulation setting
description: Verifies agent sets Modulation on DFECDR for PAM4 and uses adaptive mode with adequate gain
prompt: I have a PAM4 captured waveform in a .mat file (28 GBaud, 16 sps). Process it with an adaptive 10-tap DFE (no CTLE). Use Mode=2 with EqualizationGain=5e-3. Measure the eye after the DFE converges.

assertions.description: Sets DFECDR Modulation to 4 for PAM4
Uses Mode=2 for adaptive operation
Sets EqualizationGain higher than default for convergence
Measures eye on converged portion (skips initial transient)
Does not use SamplesPerSymbol property (read-only on DFECDR)

name: Channel impulse convolution scales by dt
description: Verifies agent multiplies channel impulse by dt when convolving with a waveform (impulse is in V/s)
prompt: I have a channel impulse response (in V/s) and a PRBS waveform. Show me how to apply the channel to get the receiver waveform using filter().

assertions.description: Multiplies impulse by dt before filtering
Uses filter() for causal convolution
Does not use conv() which doubles the length

name: serdes.Stimulus is a streaming system object
description: Verifies agent uses correct Stimulus API: SampleInterval (not SamplesPerSymbol), sample-by-sample loop, Specification='PRBS'
prompt: Generate a PRBS-13 NRZ waveform at 28 GBaud using serdes.Stimulus with 16 samples per symbol.

assertions.description: Uses SampleInterval property (not SamplesPerSymbol which doesn't exist)
Sets Specification to PRBS
Calls stim() in a loop (streaming system object, 1 sample per call)
Does not try to get multiple outputs from stim() (only 1 output)

name: Pre-loaded adapted taps fail for PAM4 time-domain
description: Verifies agent explains that stat-analysis adapted taps work for NRZ but may fail for PAM4, recommends Mode=2 adaptive DFE
prompt: I got adapted DFE taps from analysis() on my PAM4 system. Can I pre-load them into a DFECDR system object for time-domain processing?

assertions.description: Explains taps work for NRZ but may fail for PAM4
Mentions PAM4 issue with pre-loaded taps
Recommends Mode=2 adaptive DFE for PAM4 time-domain
Suggests adequate EqualizationGain for convergence

name: CTLE over-boost kills PAM4 at low loss
description: Verifies agent explains that CTLE can be counterproductive at low loss for PAM4, recommends DFE-only approach
prompt: My 56 Gbps PAM4 channel has only 10 dB loss. I added 12 dB CTLE AC gain but COM dropped. Why?

assertions.description: Explains CTLE counterproductive at low loss
Mentions CTLE boost can hurt performance
Recommends DFE-only or no CTLE at low loss
Does not suggest adding more CTLE gain

name: eyeHeight returns vector for PAM-N
description: Verifies agent explains eyeHeight returns N-1 values for PAM-N modulation, recommends min() for worst-case
prompt: I called eyeHeight on my PAM4 eyeDiagramSI and got a 3-element vector instead of a scalar. Is this a bug?

assertions.description: Explains N-1 eye openings for PAM-N
Mentions 3 eyes for PAM4 is correct
Recommends min() for worst-case eye height
Does not say this is a bug

name: Metrics.summary.eyeLinearity is lowercase
description: Verifies agent corrects the capitalization of eyeLinearity field (lowercase, not EyeLinearity)
prompt: After analysis, I tried to access sys.Metrics.summary.EyeLinearity but got an error.

assertions.description: States the field is lowercase eyeLinearity
Shows correct syntax with lowercase
Does not use incorrect EyeLinearity capitalization

name: Compliance testing with eyeMask and margin
description: Verifies agent uses eyeMask constructor and margin() method for standards-based compliance testing
prompt: Test my SerdesSystem against a compliance mask with 15 mV minimum eye height and 0.3 UI minimum width.

assertions.description: Creates eyeMask object
Sets EyeHeightThreshold property
Sets EyeWidthThreshold property
Uses margin() method with eyeDiagramSI
Mentions pass/fail criteria or margin result

name: Find maximum data rate with jitter constraint
description: Verifies agent can sweep symbol time, set JitterAndNoise with correct units, and use EH threshold to find max data rate
prompt: For a NRZ SerdesSystem with Tx_Rj=0.01 UI of jitter, a receiver with a 4-tap DFECDR, and channel loss of 20 dB at 14 GHz, find the maximum data rate where eye height at BER=1e-12 is at least 100 mV. Sweep symbol time with a coarse pass then binary search to find the threshold.

assertions.description: Generated sweep code runs without error in MATLAB
JitterAndNoise Tx_Rj set via direct scalar assignment (not SimpleJitter constructor)
Jitter value converted from UI to seconds (0.01 * symbolTime)
ChannelLossFreq explicitly set (not left at 5 GHz default)
EH read from sys.Metrics.summary.EH (not fabricated metric)
Transmitter or Receiver uses single-quoted property names

name: Bathtub curve from eye diagram
description: Verifies agent uses bathtub() method on eyeDiagramSI to generate bathtub curves
prompt: Generate a bathtub curve from my eye diagram.

assertions.description: Uses bathtub() method
Calls bathtub on eyeDiagramSI object
Explains bathtub shows BER vs timing offset
Does not fabricate non-existent bathtub analysis functions

name: AMI GetWave uses chunked for-loop
description: Verifies serdes.AMI GetWave called in a for-loop with BlockSize-length chunks, not as a single call
prompt: Run GetWave validation of my compiled Rx IBIS-AMI DLL against a Simulink waveform. I have the channel output and Rx output logged from sim().

assertions.description: Calls serdes.AMI in a for-loop with BlockSize-length chunks
Sets BlockSize property on serdes.AMI object
Sets SkipFirstBlock = false for MATLAB calling
Pads waveform to multiple of BlockSize before chunked loop
Does not pass entire waveform in a single ami() call without chunking

name: SkipFirstBlock diagnosis for MATLAB AMI calls
description: Verifies agent identifies SkipFirstBlock as the cause when first block of GetWave output is passthrough
prompt: I'm calling serdes.AMI from MATLAB to process a waveform through my compiled Rx DLL. The first 1024 samples look like unprocessed input but the rest looks correct. What's wrong?

assertions.description: Identifies SkipFirstBlock as the issue
Sets SkipFirstBlock = false
Explains default true is for Simulink internal buffering
Does not suggest the DLL is broken or data is corrupt

name: pulse2wave works for PAM4 waveforms
description: Verifies agent uses pulse2wave for PAM4 waveform generation without claiming it is NRZ-only
prompt: Generate a PAM4 time-domain waveform from a pulse response for eye diagram analysis. I have a 56 Gbps PAM4 system.

assertions.description: Uses pulse2wave for waveform generation
Does not say pulse2wave is NRZ-only or 2-level only
Does not say pulse2wave cannot handle PAM4
Provides a PRBS or symbol pattern as stimulus input
